---
title: "Carbon Intensity: 20-Year vs 100-Year Horizon"
format:
  html:
    self-contained: true
  pdf:
    fig-width: 8
    fig-height: 8
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: inline
---

```{r setup}
#| warning: false
#| message: false
knitr::opts_chunk$set(fig.width = 8, fig.height = 8, dpi = 150)
library(tidyverse)
library(patchwork)
```

```{r load}
#| warning: false
#| message: false
# Li & He (2022) Table S3 — median CI (kg CO2e/MWh) at 20-year and 100-year GWP horizon
trophic_data <- tibble(
  Category = factor(c("Oligotrophic", "Mesotrophic", "Eutrophic"),
                    levels = c("Oligotrophic", "Mesotrophic", "Eutrophic")),
  CI_20yr  = c(67.2, 116.0, 248.0),
  CI_100yr = c(55.6, 69.1, 116.7)
)

depth_data <- tibble(
  Category = factor(c("Shallow (<20 m)", "Medium (20–100 m)", "Deep (>100 m)"),
                    levels = c("Deep (>100 m)", "Medium (20–100 m)", "Shallow (<20 m)")),
  CI_20yr  = c(233.0, 114.3, 35.6),
  CI_100yr = c(124.5, 66.0, 27.7)
)

trophic_long <- trophic_data %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("CI"), names_to = "Horizon", values_to = "CI") %>%
  mutate(Horizon = factor(if_else(Horizon == "CI_20yr", "20-Year", "100-Year"), levels = c("20-Year", "100-Year")))

depth_long <- depth_data %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("CI"), names_to = "Horizon", values_to = "CI") %>%
  mutate(Horizon = factor(if_else(Horizon == "CI_20yr", "20-Year", "100-Year"), levels = c("20-Year", "100-Year")))
```

```{r plot}
#| warning: false
#| message: false
# Palette: 20-year = warmer (short-term methane impact), 100-year = baltic blue
horizon_fill <- c("20-Year" = "#c45c2a", "100-Year" = "#1c5d99")

plot_trophic <- ggplot(trophic_long, aes(x = Category, y = CI, fill = Horizon)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 80, linetype = "dashed", color = "#222222", linewidth = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = horizon_fill) +
  labs(
    title = "Carbon intensity by trophic level",
    subtitle = "Lower nutrients (Oligotrophic) = lower CI. 20-year GWP emphasizes short-term methane.",
    x = "Trophic state",
    y = "Median CI (kg CO₂e/MWh)",
    fill = "Time horizon"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

plot_depth <- ggplot(depth_long, aes(x = Category, y = CI, fill = Horizon)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 80, linetype = "dashed", color = "#222222", linewidth = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = horizon_fill) +
  labs(
    title = "Carbon intensity by reservoir depth",
    subtitle = "Deep reservoirs are much cleaner than shallow ones.",
    x = "Reservoir depth",
    y = "Median CI (kg CO₂e/MWh)",
    fill = "Time horizon"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

# Heatmaps: same data, Category × Horizon, fill = CI
heat_trophic <- ggplot(trophic_long, aes(x = Horizon, y = Category, fill = CI)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(CI)), color = "white", fontface = "bold", size = 3.2) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "viridis", name = "Median CI\n(kg CO₂e/MWh)") +
  labs(title = "Trophic level (heatmap)", x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(hjust = 0)
  )

heat_depth <- ggplot(depth_long, aes(x = Horizon, y = Category, fill = CI)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(CI)), color = "white", fontface = "bold", size = 3.2) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "viridis", name = "Median CI\n(kg CO₂e/MWh)") +
  labs(title = "Reservoir depth (heatmap)", x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(hjust = 0)
  )

(plot_trophic | plot_depth) / (heat_trophic | heat_depth) +
  plot_annotation(
    caption = "Source: Li & He (2022) Table S3. Dotted line = 80 kg CO₂e/MWh (low-carbon target)."
  ) +
  theme(plot.caption = element_text(size = 8, color = "gray50"))
```

**Takeaway:** Deep and oligotrophic reservoirs have the lowest carbon intensity; shallow and eutrophic have the highest. The 20-year GWP horizon (warmer bars) makes short-term methane impact more visible than the 100-year horizon (blue bars).
